items.correct: The zigzag movement of sediment along a coast caused by waves approaching at an angle (swash up the beach) and gravity returning water perpendicular (backwash).
items.misconception: Sand moves along the beach because tides pull it sideways.
items.random_wrong: Longshore drift is the rising of sea level over time.
items.why: Students confuse tides with waves. Longshore drift is a wave-driven process — the angle of swash vs straight-down backwash creates the zigzag, not tidal pull.

items.q: What maintains the savanna biome's mix of grass and scattered trees?
items.correct: A pronounced wet/dry season prevents continuous forest, frequent fires kill saplings, and large herbivores (e.g. elephants) suppress tree regeneration — maintaining open grass-dominated landscape.
items.misconception: The savanna is dry because it's halfway between rainforest and desert.
items.random_wrong: Savanna trees are scattered because lions eat too many of them.
items.why: It's not just rainfall — fire and herbivory are essential disturbance regimes. Without them, savannas tend to revert to woodland. This is why fire management is central to conservation.

items.q: What is the difference between the focus and epicentre of an earthquake?
items.correct: The focus (hypocentre) is the underground point where the earthquake originates; the epicentre is the point on the surface directly above the focus.
items.misconception: The focus and epicentre are the same point.
items.random_wrong: The focus is at the centre of the Earth and the epicentre is on the surface.
items.why: Students treat the two terms as synonymous or place the focus deep at the Earth's core. The focus is just below the epicentre, typically only a few to tens of kilometres deep.

items.q: What was the moment magnitude of the 2011 Tohoku earthquake?
items.why: Correct answer: 9.0. “7.0” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Approximately how many people died in the 2010 Haiti earthquake?
items.why: Correct answer: 220,000. “18,500” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In which year did the Tohoku earthquake occur?
items.why: The real figure is 2011. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
